---
title: "2.4 Nested if Statements"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Nested Branching

### One conditional can appear inside another

Nested `if` statements let a program refine a decision after the outer condition is true.

```java
if (condition1)
{
    if (condition2)
    {
        statement;
    }
}
```

The inner test is only evaluated if the outer path is reached first.

## Multiway Selection

### `else if` is the common pattern for three or more branches

![if / else if / else flowchart](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-4-nested-if-statements/Condition-three.png){fig-align="center" width="56%"}

```java
if (x < 0)
{
    System.out.println("negative");
}
else if (x == 0)
{
    System.out.println("zero");
}
else
{
    System.out.println("positive");
}
```

Only the <span class="mark">first true branch</span> runs.

## Reading Order Matters

### The sequence of conditions changes the result

In multiway selection:

::: {.tight-list}
- conditions are checked top to bottom
- once one branch runs, the rest are skipped
- broad conditions placed too early can hide later cases
:::

This is why tracing branch order is part of the lesson, not just syntax practice.

## Dangling Else

### `else` belongs to the closest unmatched `if`

```java
if (sunny)
    if (hot)
        System.out.println("Head for the beach!");
    else
        System.out.println("Bring your umbrella!");
```

The `else` above belongs to the inner `if`, regardless of indentation.

Use braces to make the intended structure explicit.

## Using Braces to Clarify Structure

### Braces remove ambiguity

```java
if (sunny)
{
    if (hot)
        System.out.println("Head for the beach!");
}
else
{
    System.out.println("Bring your umbrella!");
}
```

This is the clean classroom rule: <span class="mark">if nesting can confuse the reader, bracket it</span>.

## Classroom Task

### Adventure

![Adventure challenge](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-4-nested-if-statements/adventure.jpg){fig-align="center" width="34%"}

Retained task focus:

::: {.tight-list}
- use `else if` for multiple direction commands
- add a final `else` for invalid input
- connect each branch to a different method or location
- <span class="term">2.4.3 Coding Challenge: Adventure</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain how nested `if` statements work
- distinguish `if/else if/else` from separate unrelated `if` statements
- trace multiway selection from top to bottom
- explain the dangling-else rule
- use braces to make nested branch structure unambiguous
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
